title: myosotis discolor subsp. canariensis
intro: Myosotis discolor subsp. canariensis, also known as the Canary Island forget-me-not, is a flowering plant native to the Canary Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. The herbal preparation of Myosotis discolor subsp. canariensis typically involves making a tea by infusing the flowers in hot water, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
